title: Invoice payment terms catalogue
description: The 5 payment_terms values shipped day-1. Each maps to a due_date offset formula (issue_date + N days). 'custom' takes payment_terms_custom_days from the invoice column. Regulatory notes: some jurisdictions require specific payment terms; the tenant admin must select the appropriate default per region.

payment_terms.description: Payment due immediately on invoice issuance. due_date = issue_date. Common for one-time purchases + walk-in retail. Small tenants use this by default.
Payment due 15 days after issue. due_date = issue_date + 15 days. Common for B2C subscriptions.
Payment due 30 days after issue. due_date = issue_date + 30 days. Standard for B2B invoicing. This is the platform default.
payment_terms.regulatory_note: EU Late Payment Directive 2011/7/EU: B2B transactions default to 30 days unless contract states otherwise. Compliant.

payment_terms.description: Payment due 60 days after issue. due_date = issue_date + 60 days. Enterprise B2B with negotiated terms.
payment_terms.typical_use_case: Enterprise B2B with negotiated payment cycles
payment_terms.regulatory_note: EU Late Payment Directive: 60 days requires contractual agreement + reason. Ensure the tenant has the underlying contract documented.

payment_terms.description: Payment due N days after issue, where N is specified in payment_terms_custom_days. Range: 1-365. due_date = issue_date + payment_terms_custom_days days.
payment_terms.regulatory_note: EU Late Payment Directive: > 60 days requires 'grossly unfair to creditor' review. Tenant admin is responsible for compliance with local payment-term regulations.

notes: The tenant's default_payment_terms is a setting under invoice.default_payment_terms (default 'net_30'). Individual invoices may override.
payment_terms='custom' + payment_terms_custom_days is the ONLY case where the observer accepts a numeric override.
Dunning cadence is measured from due_date, not issue_date. A net_60 invoice's day+3 dunning notification fires 63 days after issuance.
